architecture.summary: Facts-as-Experts (FaE) extends Entities-as-Experts by adding a symbolic fact memory layer to a transformer-based language model. The system encodes contextual embeddings of text entities via intermediate transformer layers with entity memory access, then constructs a separate key-value fact memory where keys are (subject, relation) pairs and values are sets of object entities. At inference, a knowledge-enhanced query retrieves relevant facts from this symbolic memory and integrates them with contextual representations via learned mixing weights, allowing the model to condition its predictions on both neural context and symbolically manipulable knowledge.
trained_components: transformation matrices for head-pair encoding and query projection

biblio.title: Facts as Experts: Adaptable and Interpretable Neural Memory over Symbolic Knowledge

claims.statement: FaE outperforms FOFE baseline on FreebaseQA by nearly 10 points
FaE achieves 73.9% accuracy on FreebaseQA Wikidata Answerable setting
FaE substantially outperforms EmQL on WebQuestionsSP Wikidata Answerable setting (78.5% vs 74.6%)
FaE can be updated without re-training by manipulating its symbolic fact memory; the model allows adding new facts and overwriting existing ones
FaE achieves 9.3% absolute improvement when facts are injected at test time compared to the filtered setting (48.0% vs 38.7%) on FreebaseQA

When all test-set answers are replaced with plausible alternatives that contradict pretraining data, FaE recovers only 30% of questions correctly, showing roughly twice as many errors compared to unmodified evaluation, indicating that mixing between contextual representations and conflicting knowledge requires further research
FaE and EaE both answer around a quarter of answerable questions in zero-shot setting (without fine-tuning) on FreebaseQA Wikidata Answerable, with FaE showing slight advantage (26.9% vs 24.8%)
The model is pretrained on Wikipedia (30.58 million 128-token pieces, 85.58 million training examples) and Wikidata KB (1.54 million KB triples), with 500,000 training steps at batch size 2048
